own_review_points: registerArrayFunctions() calls arrayPrepend/arrayAppend/arrayReplace factory versions that are then overwritten by the Spanner-specific direct registrations in initializeFunctionRegistry — redundant
needsArrayCasting(BasicType) is duplicated verbatim across the 3 new array function classes (and existing ones) — extract to DdlTypeHelper
SpannerPostgreSQLTableExporter.isHistoryOrAuditedTable uses == reference equality on Table objects — relies on metadata identity guarantees
array_remove_index coalesce(t.idx != ?, true) preserves NULL-index rows (defensive since unnest-with-ordinality never yields null idx)
Human: SpannerPostgreSQLJsonArrayFunction could wrap in jsonb_array_elements in FROM and re-aggregate with string_agg, then cast to jsonb
notes: Four grounded good points: the redundant factory registrations overwritten by direct Spanner registrations, the verbatim needsArrayCasting duplication (DRY), the == reference-equality on Table in isHistoryOrAuditedTable, and the array_remove_index null-index handling reading. Weaker/trivial: the array_includes_nullable 'confirm EXCEPT' hedge, the docker_db.sh -gt praise, the VAR_SAMP float8 'verify' hedge, the ArrayTrimTest skip-reason suggestion. Missed the human's json_array alternative-implementation suggestion. Solid, deeper than the one-line human ref.
